name: Strawberries and Cream Poppy Seed Scones
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, stir together whipping cream and the sour cream. Cover; let stand for 4 hours at room temperature to thicken. Chill until serving time.
2. Stir together flour, brown sugar, poppyseed, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in another bowl. Cut in margarine or butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Combine the sour cream and egg yolk; add to the dry mixture. Mix just until moistened.
4. On a lightly floured surface, knead dough gently for 10 to 12 strokes.
5. Shape the dough into a 6-inch circle (1/2 inch thick) on an ungreased baking sheet. Cut into six wedges without separating them.
6. Brush tops with slightly beaten egg white and sprinkle with coarse sugar. Bake in a 425°F oven for 12 to 15 minutes.
7. Remove from the pan and cool for 5 minutes. Break scones apart.
8. To serve, stir marmalade into cream mixture. If necessary, stir in about 1 tablespoon milk to achieve desired consistency. Toss berries with liqueur or juice.
9. Cut scones in half, place bottoms on six dessert plates, divide berries among each serving, and top with cream mixture and scone tops. Makes 6 servings.
Nutrition facts per serving: 3 16 cal., 19 g total fat (9 g sat. fat), 75 mg chol., 292 mg sodium, 32 g carbo., 2 g fiber, 4 g pro. Daily Values: 25% vit. A, 48% vit. C.
